description: Parts are 'koru' when their differences fit into a pleasing or workable whole. Voices can be harmonious without sharing one pitch, and a community can remain harmonious while people disagree. 'weilo' (balanced) concerns equilibrium or proportion; 'koru' concerns the relation among the parts.
articulatory_notes: Close the back of the tongue against the soft palate for 'k' and release into rounded true-mid back 'o'. Make a tap or short trill at the alveolar ridge for 'r', then raise the back of the tongue and tighten the lip rounding for high-back 'u'. Stress falls on 'ko'.
sound_symbolism: A firm opening closure releases into two rounded vowels, with the tap of 'r' joining the syllables. The contour can feel like distinct parts settling into one voiced shape.
usage_notes: The intransitive verb 'lei' says that voices or parts harmonize. Transitive 'shemoli' tunes one thing to another, and 'koru' describes the resulting relation.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: A railing and the doorway beside it may differ in scale while answering one another in line. 'koru' names that relation among parts.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Harmony in dialogue does not require one voice or the disappearance of disagreement. The word can describe a workable relation among people who remain distinct.

semantic_domains.aesthetic: parts perceived as fitting together into a pleasing whole
semantic_domains.community: different participants maintaining a workable relation
